mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing sector and overall economic growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month for 3 consecutive months but the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than half of those months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a threshold level signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 5.5% but the S&P 500 5-day return is negative in at least 3 out of the next 5 instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) in conjunction with near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an erosion of real interest rates, raising expectations of future monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the short to medium term.
failure_condition: If the combination of rising CPI and low FEDFUNDS does not produce a negative average 20-day SPX return over 10 consecutive events, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above the 1,000,000 unit threshold indicates a robust housing sector recovery, stimulating construction activity and related industries, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1000 but the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of such occurrences, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The impact of industrial production (INDPRO) growth on equities is regime-dependent: when the unemployment rate is high (slack), rising IP signals expansion and positive returns; when unemployment is low (full employment), rising IP may signal overheating and negative returns. In the current slack regime (UNRATE >=5%), direction is positive.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX 20-day returns does not significantly differ (p < 0.10) between the two UNRATE regimes over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job creation (PAYEMS) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy consumer sector driving economic growth, which translates into positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200,000 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence of high payroll gains and high retail sales growth is not followed by a positive average 20-day SPX return over at least 8 observations, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) provides a broad measure of economic activity; sustained positive GDP growth indicates a healthy expansion, supporting corporate profits and equity prices over a similar quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the quarterly GDP growth rate and the subsequent 60-day SPX return show a negative correlation over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above the zero lower bound signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, compressing valuations through higher discount rates and leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If the first rate hike (FEDFUNDS > 0.25) is followed by a positive 5-day SPX return in more than half of historical episodes, the hypothesis is falsified.
